messages.author: swarm
messages.text: This ticket needs your approval before it can proceed. Reply here with "approve" to approve it.

messages.author: swarm
messages.text:
What it is: The BL-413/BL-458 /tmp sweeps are production no-ops: they bound the SCAN, not DELETES (bounded-scan wedge)
What it solves: EXPEDITE (Operator directive, 2026-07-16): a real, tracked bug against
just-shipped work — spec ahead of the backlog-root queue, promote ahead of the
paused queue. When the fix lands, the production numbers must actually fall
(orphan process count and /tmp entry count), not just a fixture.
How it works: A reapable entry ordered after the per-tick scan cap is still removed within a bounded number of ticks (bound DELETES, not SCAN) — proven for BOTH the stale-dir sweep and the orphan-process reaper

messages.author: swarm
messages.text: BL-460 - The BL-413/BL-458 /tmp sweeps are production no-ops: they bound the SCAN, not DELETES (bounded-scan wedge) is complete.
